channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Sheriff says 3 kids found 'basically abandoned' for years at suburban Detroit home
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — Donations of clothes and money are pouring in for three children who were “basically abandoned” by their mother for years, living alone in a suburban Detroit home among trash and feces, a sheriff said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ED WHITE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Questions emerge about what may have caused Delta plane to burst into flames and flip over
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Investigators will consider the weather conditions, as well as the possibility of human error or an aircraft malfunction as they try to determine why a Delta Air Lines jet burst into flames and flipped upside down as it tried to land in Toronto, aviation experts said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW and MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump signs order to study how to expand IVF and calls for 'radical transparency' from government
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President Donald Trump on Tuesday signed an executive order to study how to expand access to in vitro fertilization and make it more affordable.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE and MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump administration fires 1,000 workers at National Park Service, raises maintenance concerns
item.description: The Trump administration has fired about 1,000 newly hired National Park Service employees who maintain and clean parks, educate visitors and perform other functions as part of its broad-based effort to downsize government.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Daly, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: How National Park staffing cuts could impact your next visit
item.description: While thousands of seasonal staff are expected to be hired, the loss of other employees could make your next national park visit feel different.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Border arrests plummet 39% in January in an early gauge of Trump's immigration policies
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Arrests for illegal border crossings from Mexico plummeted 39% in January from a month earlier, authorities said Tuesday, an early gauge of President Donald Trump's immigration crackdown.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trails, bathrooms, wait times: How National Park staffing cuts could impact your next visit
item.description: While thousands of seasonal staff are expected to be hired, the loss of other employees could make your next national park visit feel different.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge questions motives for Trump's order banning transgender troops
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Tuesday questioned President Donald Trump's motives for issuing an executive order that calls for banning transgender troops from serving in the U.S. military, describing a portion of the directive as “frankly ridiculous.” U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Leonard Peltier leaves prison after Biden commuted his sentence in the killing of two FBI agents
item.description: SUMTERVILLE, Fla. (AP) — Native American activist Leonard Peltier was released from a Florida prison on Tuesday, weeks after then-President Joe Biden angered law enforcement officials by commuting his life sentence to home confinement in the 1975 killings of two FBI agents.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM LEE BREWER and CURT ANDERSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Dozens of police recruits nationwide have died while training in last decade: probe
item.description: At least 29 recruits died during basic training at law enforcement academies around the country in the last decade, an AP investigation found.
item.dc:creator: By RYAN J. FOLEY Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The pope has pneumonia in both lungs but remains in good spirits, Vatican says
item.description: Francis was admitted to Rome’s Gemelli hospital in a “fair” condition on Friday after a weeklong bout of bronchitis worsened.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Leader of cultlike 'Zizian' group linked to 6 killings in multiple states ordered held without bail
item.description: The Zizians have been tied to the killing of U.S. Border Patrol Agent David Maland near the Canadian border in January and five other homicides in Vermont, Pennsylvania and California.
item.dc:creator: Amy Larson, NewsNation

item.title: Fugitive jailed after melting hospital bed rail to escape
item.description: A man who was wanted for allegedly trying to shoot a gas station employee last year is now behind bars.
item.dc:creator: Megan Fayard, NewsNation

item.title: Virginia school districts investigated for transgender support policies
item.description: The Education Department is investigating whether five Northern Virginia school districts violated Title IX.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, NewsNation

item.title: Judge refuses to block DOGE’s access to student borrower data
item.description: A judge declined to block Elon Musk’s DOGE from gaining access to Education Department data on student borrowers.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Delphi murders: Gull rules against defense motions seeking to reexamine Richard Allen's conviction
item.description: DELPHI, Ind. -- Special Judge Fran Gull denied recent motions from Richard Allen’s attorneys seeking to reexamine his conviction last year in the Delphi murders case. In an order dated Feb. 14, Gull denied the defense’s Verified Motion to Correct Error (filed on Jan.
item.dc:creator: Matt Adams, NewsNation

item.title: Social Security chief steps down over DOGE access to recipient data: Reports
item.description: The Social Security Administration’s acting leader stepped down from her role over requests from the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) to access recipient data, according to several reports.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Columbine High School shooting survivor dies nearly 26 years after massacre
item.description: Anne Marie Hochhalter, who was shot and paralyzed in the Columbine High School mass shooting in 1999, died on Sunday of natural causes, Fox News Digital has learned. She was 43.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Utah mom accused of killing National Guard husband enters plea as police search for body
item.description: Utah mom Jennifer Gledhill, who is accused of killing her husband in September 2024, has pleaded not guilty as the search for her husband's body continues.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump administration gives schools a deadline to end DEI programs or risk losing federal money
item.description: The Trump administration is giving America’s schools and universities two weeks to eliminate diversity initiatives or risk losing federal money, raising the stakes in the president’s fight against “wokeness.”.
item.dc:creator: Collin Binkley, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: How better water systems can help a city survive the next firestorm
item.description: LOUISVILLE, Colorado (AP) — Santa Rosa. Paradise. Boulder County. Lahaina. Los Angeles. All are places that have shown that American cities and their water systems weren't built to withstand wildfire, experts say. Hydrants trickled. Pumps and treatment plants lost power.
item.dc:creator: BRITTANY PETERSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Boy killed during Valentine's date on Santana Row in San Jose ID'd
item.description: Gutierrez was "attacked by a group of juveniles while he was on a date with his girlfriend to celebrate Valentine's Day.
item.dc:creator: Rob Nesbitt, NewsNation

item.title: 4 NYC deputy mayors resign after meeting with Adams
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Four high-level officials in Mayor Eric Adams' administration resigned Monday after expressing concerns about the mayor's relationship with the Trump Administration, according to City Hall and sources familiar with the matter.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, NewsNation

item.title: New York City doctor convicted of $24M medical fraud
item.description: A jury convicted Queens doctor Alexander Baldonado for a plot costing Medicare over $24 million and taking cash for ordering fake lab tests and orthotic braces.
item.dc:creator: Johan Sheridan, NewsNation

item.title: New York City Mayor Eric Adams will face judge who will decide whether to dismiss his criminal case
item.description: New York City Mayor Eric Adams will face the federal judge on Wednesday who will decide whether to dismiss corruption charges against him after lawyers explain the abrupt change in position just weeks before an April trial.
item.dc:creator: Larry Neumeister, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Columbine survivor Anne Marie Hochhalter dies decades after shooting
item.description: Anne Marie Hochhalter, a survivor of the Columbine High School mass shooting, died Sunday, her former principal, Frank DeAngelis, told Nexstar's KDVR on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, Fox 5

item.title: White House says Elon Musk is not part of DOGE
item.description: The White House said Monday that Elon Musk is technically not part of the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), even though the tech billionaire is said to be leading its sweeping cost-cutting efforts.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, WFLA, NBC

item.title: All aboard Delta flight survive after plane flips while landing in Toronto
item.description: Communications between the tower and pilot were normal on approach and it's not clear what went so drastically wrong when the plane touched down.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Suspect in Tupac Shakur killing seeks to delay trial as defense identifies new witnesses
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Attorneys for the man suspected of killing rap icon Tupac Shakur want to delay next month’s trial, saying more time is needed for investigative work to ensure that Duane “Keffe D” Davis gets a fair trial.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Sheryl Crow sells Tesla to donate funds to embattled NPR
item.description: “Money donated to NPR, which is under threat by President Musk, in hopes that the truth will continue to find its way to those willing to know the truth,” the country artist wrote.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US Army bans transgender people from enlisting
item.description: The new policies, effective immediately, could affect more than 10,000 active duty troops, per AP estimates.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, ABC 4

item.title: 83 percent say president is required to follow Supreme Court rulings: Survey
item.description: Just over half, 51 percent, of Americans approve of the job the Supreme Court is doing, which is the highest amount of support it’s received in the last three years.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WGN 9

item.title: Why you shouldn't leave your windshield wipers up during every snowstorm
item.description: Propping windshield wipers up before a snowstorm is a common routine for many car owners, but according to AAA, it's not always the best practice.
item.dc:creator: Maddie Rhodes, WGN 9

item.title: Zelenskyy to Vance: Ukraine wants 'security guarantees' as Trump seeks to end Ukraine-Russia war
item.description: MUNICH (AP) — Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Friday that his country wants “security guarantees” before any talks with Russia, as the Trump administration presses both countries to find a quick endgame to the three-year war.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, EMMA BURROWS and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump signs executive order stripping funds from schools requiring COVID-19 vaccines
item.description: President Trump signed an executive order Friday to defund schools and other education agencies that require COVID-19 vaccines for students and staff. Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WGN 9

item.title: US Park Service erases the word 'transgender' from website commemorating Stonewall riot
item.description: References to transgender people were removed Thursday from a National Park Service website for the Stonewall National Monument, a park and visitor center in New York that commemorates a 1969 riot that became a pivotal moment for the LGBTQ+ rights movement.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN HAIGH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man to plead guilty to 2023 shooting of Black teen Ralph Yarl, 2 people familiar with case tell AP
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Mo. (AP) — An 86-year-old Kansas City man will plead guilty Friday to the 2023 shooting of Ralph Yarl, a Black honor student who rang the man’s doorbell by mistake, two people familiar with the case told The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: KIA BREAUX and HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, ABC 4
